project.name: flowsphere
project.description: Automate sequential HTTP workflows through JSON configuration. Create, edit and execute via visual Studio or CLI. Supports conditional execution, response chaining, user input prompts, and validations. Note: this project has over 100 source files; consider scoping analysis to a subdirectory for faster results.

nodes.name: conditions.js
nodes.summary: Conditional execution engine that evaluates whether a step should run based on previous responses, global variables, or user input. Supports equality, existence, and numeric comparison conditions combined with AND logic.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large evaluateCondition function handles many condition types via branching; all condition values support variable substitution before evaluation.

nodes.name: evaluateCondition
nodes.summary: Evaluates a single condition against the execution context, resolving the source (node response, variable, or input), applying variable substitution, and checking the requested comparison (equals, exists, greaterThan, etc.).

nodes.name: evaluateConditions
nodes.summary: Evaluates an array of conditions with AND logic, returning a skip decision and detailed reason when any condition fails.

nodes.name: executor.js
nodes.summary: Main execution engine that orchestrates the entire HTTP sequence: loads and validates config, merges defaults, collects user input, evaluates conditions, executes requests, validates responses, and maintains state across steps.
nodes.languageNotes: runSequence is a long async orchestration loop coordinating substitution, conditions, HTTP, and validation modules; responses[] array maintains cross-step state.

nodes.name: mergeWithDefaults
nodes.summary: Merges a step's configuration with global defaults, prepending baseUrl to relative URLs and combining headers, timeout, and validations.

nodes.name: promptUserInput
nodes.summary: Collects interactive user input from the terminal for a step's userPrompts using readline, returning a map of variable names to entered values.

nodes.name: executeStep
nodes.summary: Executes a single HTTP step by applying variable substitution with tracking and delegating to the HTTP client's executeRequest.

nodes.name: runSequence
nodes.summary: Orchestrates the full sequence run: reads and validates config, iterates nodes applying defaults, conditions, prompts, execution, validation, browser launch, and execution logging.

nodes.name: http-client.js
nodes.summary: HTTP request handling layer wrapping axios, providing timeout support, automatic JSON parsing of responses, and error handling for network failures and timeouts.

nodes.name: executeRequest
nodes.summary: Executes a single HTTP request via axios with configurable method, body, and timeout, parsing the JSON response and surfacing timeout and network errors with clear messages.

nodes.name: substitution.js
nodes.summary: Variable substitution engine that replaces dynamic placeholders ($guid, $timestamp), global variables, user input, and response references within strings and recursively within objects.
nodes.languageNotes: Substitution order is enforced: dynamic placeholders -> global vars -> user input -> response references; substitution tracking records original, value, type, and path for UI display.

nodes.name: replaceDynamicPlaceholders
nodes.summary: Replaces Postman-style dynamic placeholders {{ $guid }} and {{ $timestamp }} in a string, generating a fresh UUID for each guid occurrence and a shared timestamp.

nodes.name: substituteVariables
nodes.summary: Replaces all placeholder syntaxes (dynamic, global variables, user input, response references) in a string in the correct order, optionally tracking each substitution for reporting.

nodes.name: substituteInObject
nodes.summary: Recursively applies variable substitution across all string values in objects and arrays, preserving structure.

nodes.name: substituteWithTracking
nodes.summary: Convenience wrapper that performs object-wide substitution while collecting a tracking record of all substitutions applied.

nodes.name: utils.js
nodes.summary: Shared utility module providing UUID and timestamp generation, JSON file I/O, terminal colorization, JSON path extraction, deep object merging, and duration/log formatting used across the execution engine.
nodes.languageNotes: extractValue implements a custom JSON path resolver supporting array indexing (.[0]) and length queries (. | length); generateUUID uses native crypto.randomUUID.

nodes.name: readJSONFile
nodes.summary: Reads and parses a JSON file from disk, returning the parsed object or throwing a descriptive error if the file is missing or malformed.

nodes.name: writeJSONFile
nodes.summary: Serializes data to a JSON file, creating the parent directory if it does not exist.

nodes.name: extractValue
nodes.summary: Extracts a value from an object using a dot-notation JSON path, supporting array indexing and length queries used for response references and validations.

nodes.name: deepMerge
nodes.summary: Recursively merges a source object into a target object, used for merging step configuration with defaults.

nodes.name: getLogTimestamp
nodes.summary: Builds a zero-padded YYYYMMDD_HHMMSS timestamp string used for naming execution log files.

nodes.name: flowsphere.js
nodes.summary: CLI entry point for FlowSphere that parses command-line arguments (--start-step, --version, --help, studio), validates and runs sequence configs, and launches an Express-based Studio server exposing execute, validate, flow-runner SSE, and template endpoints.
nodes.languageNotes: The launchStudio function spans ~1000 lines and registers all Express routes inline, including a Server-Sent Events (SSE) endpoint for streaming flow-runner execution and a pendingInputRequests map for interactive user-input prompts.

nodes.name: showHelp
nodes.summary: Prints the CLI usage banner, available commands, options, and examples to stdout.

nodes.name: launchStudio
nodes.summary: Boots the FlowSphere Studio Express server: registers all HTTP routes (single-step execute, submit-input, save-log, try-it-out, flow-runner SSE stream, config validation, templates), opens the browser, and handles graceful shutdown.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses Server-Sent Events (res.writeHead with text/event-stream) to stream step-by-step execution and a pendingInputRequests Map to await interactive user input mid-flow.

nodes.name: main
nodes.summary: Top-level CLI dispatcher that interprets flags, resolves the config path, validates the config via validateConfig, and orchestrates sequence execution with runSequence plus optional log saving.

nodes.name: config-validator.js
nodes.summary: Comprehensive config validation engine that checks structure and values of a FlowSphere config: top-level shape, variables, defaults, nodes (id/method/url/headers/body), user prompts, conditions, validation rules, and {{ }} placeholder references, returning categorized errors.
nodes.languageNotes: Distinguishes structure-only vs values-only validation via options, and categorizes each error so callers (CLI, Studio) can filter by error category.

nodes.name: validateConfig
nodes.summary: Top-level config validator that orchestrates variable, defaults, and per-node validation, detects duplicate node IDs, and aggregates categorized errors. Supports structureOnly and valuesOnly modes.

nodes.name: validateDefaults
nodes.summary: Validates the defaults section: baseUrl format, timeout, headers shape, and the array of default validation rules.

nodes.name: validateNode
nodes.summary: Validates an individual node: required id/method/url, HTTP method whitelist, relative-vs-absolute URL rules, header/body shape, content-type consistency, user prompts, conditions, validation rules, and launchBrowser path.

nodes.name: validateConditions
nodes.summary: Validates a node's conditions array is well-formed and delegates each entry to validateCondition.

nodes.name: validateCondition
nodes.summary: Validates a single condition object, ensuring exactly one source (node/variable/input/legacy response) is present and routing to the matching source-specific validator.

nodes.name: validateNodeCondition
nodes.summary: Validates a node-sourced condition: referenced node id exists, field path format, and that the comparison operator is one of the supported set.

nodes.name: validateLegacyResponseCondition
nodes.summary: Validates the deprecated response-style condition shape, checking field path and comparison operators while preserving backward compatibility.

nodes.name: validateValidationRule
nodes.summary: Validates a single validation rule object (httpStatusCode or jsonpath criteria), ensuring jsonpath format and supported comparison operators.

nodes.name: checkForMalformedPlaceholders
nodes.summary: Recursively scans strings, arrays, and objects for malformed {{ }} substitution placeholders and records errors.

nodes.name: validatePlaceholders
nodes.summary: Extracts all {{ }} placeholders from a serialized node and validates response/vars/input references against known node ids and supported syntax.

nodes.name: formatErrors
nodes.summary: Formats an array of validation errors into a human-readable string for CLI output.

nodes.name: validator.js
nodes.summary: Response validation engine that evaluates an HTTP response against a node's validation rules (HTTP status code plus jsonpath criteria such as exists, equals, greaterThan/lessThan) and returns structured pass/fail results.

nodes.name: validateResponse
nodes.summary: Validates an HTTP response against its node's validation array, checking status code and jsonpath criteria (exists, equals/notEquals, numeric comparisons) and producing per-check results plus an aggregate error message.

nodes.name: test-validator-categories.js
nodes.summary: Standalone test script that verifies validateConfig correctly separates structure errors from value errors across full, structureOnly, and valuesOnly validation modes using a config seeded with a duplicate id, a bad reference, and a missing method.

nodes.name: CLAUDE.md
nodes.summary: Agent guidance and authoritative architecture reference for FlowSphere, documenting the Node.js core modules (executor, substitution, http-client, validator, conditions, logger, utils), config file format, Studio UI patterns, Postman integration, and development/documentation conventions.
nodes.languageNotes: Large Markdown knowledge base (39 sections) acting as the project's single source of truth for architecture and contribution rules.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: User-facing introduction to FlowSphere covering installation, quick-start CLI and Studio workflows, the programmatic API, config validation, the configuration format, variable substitution, conditional execution, and response validation.

nodes.name: ROADMAP.md
nodes.summary: Feature roadmap enumerating planned and completed FlowSphere capabilities (JSON beautify, response header access, OpenAPI/Postman import/export, OAuth callback capture, multi-environment variable groups) with per-feature summaries and a contribution process.

nodes.name: package.json
nodes.summary: npm package manifest for FlowSphere defining the flowsphere CLI bin entry pointing at bin/flowsphere.js, runtime dependencies (axios, express, open), the main module, scripts, engines, and publishing metadata.
nodes.languageNotes: Defines a single bin alias (flowsphere) and restricts engines to Node.js >= 14.17.0; runtime deps are axios, express, and open.

nodes.name: enhanced-postman-import.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for improving Postman collection import, covering order-of-appearance import without numbering, better variable resolution, and import preview, with a Postman-to-FlowSphere feature mapping and phased rollout.

nodes.name: execution-log-visualizer.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for a visual tool that turns JSON execution logs into a summary panel, timeline/waterfall view, expandable step cards, filters, and exportable reports across CLI and Studio.

nodes.name: export-to-postman.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for exporting FlowSphere configs to Postman collection/environment files, including validation-to-test-script and condition-to-pre-request-script conversion and a phased implementation plan.

nodes.name: flow-runner-execution-controls.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for Flow Runner execution-control modes (Full Speed, Step-by-Step, Auto-Step with configurable delay) plus stop/cancel functionality, documenting the design evolution away from pause/resume.

nodes.name: multi-environment-variable-groups.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for environment-scoped variable groups (dev/staging/prod) with config format, CLI/Studio integration, variable resolution and inheritance, security redaction, and a migration guide from the single variables section.

nodes.name: node-templates-import-system.md
nodes.summary: Technical design for a node-templates and import system featuring a two-button architecture, template storage structure and format, and smart features like variable auto-creation, ID auto-rename, and node placement with highlighting.

nodes.name: oauth-callback-capture.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for capturing OAuth redirect callbacks in Studio, covering callback URL registration, automatic callback handling, client-server communication, state validation, timeouts, and multi-session support.

nodes.name: response-header-access.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for accessing HTTP response headers and status in substitution, validations, and conditions, including new response-storage structure, axios mapping, regex patterns, and breaking-change migration notes.

nodes.name: swagger-openapi-import.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive feature specification for importing Swagger 2.0 and OpenAPI 3.0 specs into FlowSphere configs, covering endpoint discovery, schema-to-validation mapping, auth conversion, $ref resolution, and reuse of Postman-import patterns.

nodes.name: visual-workflow-storytelling-export.md
nodes.summary: Feature specification for animated visual storytelling of workflow execution, with Story/Debug modes, an interactive timeline scrubber, variable-substitution narration, and self-contained HTML export of API integration flows.

nodes.name: enhanced-postman-import-status.md
nodes.summary: Implementation status tracker for the Enhanced Postman Import feature, documenting three completed phases (order-of-appearance import, environment file support, import preview), known auth limitations, and the files modified per phase.

nodes.name: phase1-stop-implementation-status.md
nodes.summary: Implementation status for Phase 1 of the Flow Runner execution controls, documenting the graceful stop/cancel feature across client (flow-runner.js) and server (flowsphere.js), including bug fixes, testing, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: phase2-step-by-step-implementation-status.md
nodes.summary: Implementation status for Phase 2 of the Flow Runner execution controls, documenting step-by-step execution with client and server changes, UI/UX flow, key implementation details, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: phase3-auto-step-implementation-status.md
nodes.summary: Implementation status for Phase 3 of the Flow Runner execution controls, documenting auto-step execution as a client-only change in flow-runner.js with UI/UX flow, implementation details, and acceptance criteria.

nodes.name: API_FlowSphere_Node_Execution_Branding_Prompt.md
nodes.summary: AI prompt specifying the branded UI copy and modal states for the 'Execute Node Separately' feature, including primary/re-run buttons and the manual-input-required modal.

nodes.name: API_FlowSphere_Theme_Sonnet_4.5.md
nodes.summary: Comprehensive branding and theming prompt defining the FlowSphere color palette, HTTP verb colors, button variants, typography, and layout rules for the Studio UI.

nodes.name: API_FlowSphere_Theme_Sonnet_4.5_v2.md
nodes.summary: Revised v2 of the FlowSphere theming prompt, extending the original palette and button specs with product naming and tagline guidance.

nodes.name: FlowSphere_Load_Config_Rebrand_Prompt.md
nodes.summary: AI prompt to rebrand the Load Config modal, covering the primary button, modal header/sections (start from scratch vs. import), visual details, empty-state alignment, and microcopy.

nodes.name: FlowSphere_Studio_Logo_Adjustment_Prompt.md
nodes.summary: AI prompt to adjust the FlowSphere Studio logo's size and placement, with implementation goals for scaling, responsive behavior, theme adaptation, and optional animation.

nodes.name: claude_bash_http_sequence_prompt.md
nodes.summary: Master AI prompt that originally specified the Bash HTTP sequence runner, defining the config file format, implementation details, and expected deliverables.

nodes.name: claude_postman_to_bash_config_prompt.md
nodes.summary: AI prompt for populating an existing app config from a Postman collection, defining input files, execution rules, the conversion task, and quality requirements.

nodes.name: flow_control_panel_branding.md
nodes.summary: Branding prompt for the Flow Execution Mode control panel, defining the modal title and three execution options (Full Throttle, Precision Mode, Auto-Step) with voice and tone.

nodes.name: flow_execution_modal_lifecycle.md
nodes.summary: Prompt defining the branded button lifecycle for the Flow Execution modal across four stages (running, input required, success, failure) with behavioral design principles.

nodes.name: flow_execution_modes_branding.md
nodes.summary: Branding prompt enhancing the Flow Execution Mode modal with Step-by-Step (manual precision) and Auto-Step (rhythmic automation) modes plus voice and visual identity notes.

nodes.name: change-impact-guide.md
nodes.summary: Authoritative "chain of events" reference mapping every change type (new config field, validation type, condition source, substitution syntax, API endpoint, Studio feature, node template) to the exact set of files that must be updated, plus codebase structure, config schema, file-relationship flows, and a testing checklist.
nodes.languageNotes: Organized as 10 numbered change-type chains plus quick-reference and invariants sections, designed so agents can locate all affected files for any modification.

nodes.name: config-validation-system.md
nodes.summary: Technical design of the two-tier (structure + value) config validation system implemented in lib/config-validator.js, covering validation flow, CLI and Studio integration points, inline JSON validation, result/error data structures, and the surfacing of errors through the Studio UI.

nodes.name: core-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Primary architecture reference explaining how FlowSphere works: config is stored in studio/js/state.js (never window.config), Studio mutates config and re-renders via renderEditor(), and CLI/Studio/Try-it-Out all reuse the lib/executor.js engine. Includes per-module breakdowns, execution flows (CLI and SSE), the context object, and common mistakes to avoid.
nodes.languageNotes: Leads with a "Quick Start for Agents" and five common-mistake anti-patterns (e.g., accessing window.config, forgetting renderEditor(), duplicating executor logic) to prevent recurring agent errors.

nodes.name: flow-execution-architecture.md
nodes.summary: Concise description of the client-server execution lifecycle for the Studio Flow Runner, covering initialization, the per-step SSE execution loop, normal completion, and graceful interruption handling.

nodes.name: modal-system.md
nodes.summary: Architecture of the Studio modal system: a global configuration object and helper enforcing "sticky" modals (static backdrop, no ESC dismiss) across flow-runner.js, try-it-out.js, and import-nodes.js, with developer guidelines, common patterns, and troubleshooting.

nodes.name: toggleable-features-guide.md
nodes.summary: Guide for making new features toggleable via four patterns: Feature Registry (studio/js/core/feature-registry.js), built-in localStorage toggles, config-level toggles validated in lib/config-validator.js, and optional node properties executed in lib/executor.js. Includes naming conventions, a testing checklist, a pattern-selection table, and real-world examples.
nodes.languageNotes: Four implementation patterns mapped to scope (UI feature, built-in behavior, config-level, node-level), each illustrated with a real codebase example such as autocomplete, theme switcher, debug logging, and user prompts.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index and usage guide for the ready-to-run example FlowSphere configurations, with a table describing each config file, its purpose, and key features, plus instructions for running them via CLI or loading them in the visual editor.

nodes.name: config-comprehensive-demo.json
nodes.summary: Comprehensive 12-node demo workflow exercising every FlowSphere feature: global variables, defaults, user input prompts, conditional execution, multiple validation types, variable substitution, dynamic placeholders, and all HTTP methods.
nodes.languageNotes: Has top-level variables, defaults, and nodes sections; the richest example, covering global vars, conditions, and the full validation matrix.

nodes.name: config-ids-token.json
nodes.summary: Single-step token acquisition workflow that POSTs a form-urlencoded body to an identity server to obtain an access token, with response validations.

nodes.name: config-oauth-example.json
nodes.summary: OAuth authentication workflow example demonstrating browser auto-launch, interactive user input prompts, and form-urlencoded request bodies across an authorization sequence.

nodes.name: config-simple.json
nodes.summary: Beginner-friendly starter workflow using the public JSONPlaceholder API to demonstrate a basic request sequence with defaults and validations, requiring no setup.

nodes.name: config-test-features.json
nodes.summary: Feature demonstration workflow showcasing interactive user input prompt collection and variable substitution into request bodies and URLs.

nodes.name: config-user-input.json
nodes.summary: User input demonstration workflow showing how to collect user-provided values via prompts and reference them with the {{ .input.x }} substitution syntax in subsequent requests.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Full-featured example workflow combining authentication, response validation, defaults inheritance, and response reference substitution across a multi-step HTTP sequence.

nodes.name: Endpoint 3.postman_collection.json
nodes.summary: Sample Postman collection fixture with 12 sequential requests (3.01-3.12) against the dummy.restapiexample.com create endpoint, used to exercise the Postman-to-FlowSphere converter.
nodes.languageNotes: Postman Collection v2.1 schema; numeric-prefixed request names drive the converter's ordering logic.

nodes.name: Fake API.postman_collection.json
nodes.summary: Sample Postman collection fixture with Meta and Execute folders (15 requests) targeting a Fake API via the {{base_url}} environment variable, used as input for the Postman-to-FlowSphere converter.
nodes.languageNotes: Postman Collection v2.1 with nested item folders and {{base_url}} variable references resolved from the companion environment files.

nodes.name: FakeAPI Heroku.postman_environment.json
nodes.summary: Sample Postman environment fixture defining base_url, username, and password variables (values blank) for the Fake API collection, used to test environment-variable resolution in the converter.
nodes.languageNotes: Postman environment export with empty value fields, exercising the converter's handling of unset variables.

nodes.name: FakeAPI Heroku.postman_environment_with_values.json
nodes.summary: Sample Postman environment fixture with populated base_url, username, and password values for the Fake API collection, used to test variable substitution in the Postman-to-FlowSphere converter.
nodes.languageNotes: Postman environment export with filled values; counterpart to the blank-value environment file for converter testing.

nodes.name: api-key.json
nodes.summary: Reusable Studio node template that injects a static API key into request headers via the {{ .vars.apiKey }} substitution, producing a single authenticated GET request. Imported into configs through the Studio node-templates import system.
nodes.languageNotes: Template declares requiredVariables (apiKey) consumed by the X-API-Key header through FlowSphere's {{ .vars.* }} substitution syntax.

nodes.name: bearer-token.json
nodes.summary: Reusable Studio node template that performs a username/password login and validates that a bearer token is present in the response. Imported into configs through the Studio node-templates import system.
nodes.languageNotes: Declares requiredVariables (apiUsername, apiPassword) and asserts token extraction with a jsonpath '.token' exists validation.

nodes.name: oauth-flow.json
nodes.summary: Reusable Studio node template implementing a two-step OAuth 2.0 authorization-code flow: requests an authorization URL (launching the browser), then exchanges a user-supplied code for an access token. Imported into configs through the Studio node-templates import system.
nodes.languageNotes: Combines launchBrowser, userPrompts (authCode input), and chained validations across two nodes; declares requiredVariables clientId, clientSecret, and redirectUri.

nodes.name: custom-input.json
nodes.summary: Reusable FlowSphere Studio node template providing a generic single-step pattern that prompts the user for a custom value, substitutes it into a POST body via {{ .input.customValue }}, and validates a 200 status.
nodes.languageNotes: Node template carrying templateName/description/category metadata plus a nodes array consumed by Studio's import-nodes loader.

nodes.name: username-password.json
nodes.summary: Reusable FlowSphere Studio node template for interactive credential collection: prompts for username and password, posts them to /auth/login, and validates a 200 status plus the presence of a .token field.
nodes.languageNotes: Demonstrates combining userPrompts with body substitution and a jsonpath existence validation on the login response.

nodes.name: verification-code.json
nodes.summary: Reusable FlowSphere Studio node template for 2FA/MFA flows that prompts for a verification code, posts it to /auth/verify, and validates a 200 status plus a .verified equals true assertion.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses an equality jsonpath validation to confirm the verification step succeeded.

nodes.name: multi-field.json
nodes.summary: Reusable Studio node template demonstrating multiple jsonpath validations on a single GET request, combining existence, inequality, and numeric range checks (greaterThanOrEqual/lessThanOrEqual) on user profile fields.

nodes.name: paginated-response.json
nodes.summary: Reusable Studio node template that validates a paginated API response, asserting array length bounds via the `| length` jsonpath operator and checking pagination metadata fields like totalPages and currentPage.

nodes.name: status-token-extract.json
nodes.summary: Reusable Studio node template for a common auth login flow, validating HTTP status plus token existence and a positive expiresIn value on the POST /auth/login response.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Index of FlowSphere test fixture configurations, describing what feature each config exercises (variables, validations, defaults, comparisons) and how to run them for regression and edge-case testing.

nodes.name: config-test-comparisons.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that exercises numeric comparison validations (greaterThan, lessThan, greaterThanOrEqual, lessThanOrEqual) against API responses.

nodes.name: config-test-condition-variables.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that exercises variable substitution inside conditional execution rules, verifying that global vars, input, and response references resolve within condition values.

nodes.name: config-test-defaults.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that exercises defaults merging behavior, verifying that baseUrl, headers, timeout, and default validations are correctly merged into each step.

nodes.name: config-test-duplicate-ids.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config containing duplicate node id values, used to verify the config validator detects and reports duplicate step identifiers.

nodes.name: config-test-invalid-json-body.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config with malformed or invalid request body structures, used to verify the config validator catches invalid JSON body definitions.

nodes.name: config-test-malformed-placeholders.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config containing malformed variable substitution placeholders, used to verify graceful handling of broken {{ }} syntax during substitution.

nodes.name: config-test-multiple-validations.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that applies multiple JSON path validations on a single step, verifying combined existence, equality, and numeric criteria evaluation.

nodes.name: config-test-persistent-input.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that exercises persistence of user input prompts across steps, verifying that collected input values remain accessible to later nodes.

nodes.name: config-test-skip-defaults.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that verifies conditional skipping interacts correctly with default validations, ensuring skipped steps maintain array indexing and do not inherit unexpected defaults.

nodes.name: config-test-validation-errors.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that intentionally triggers validation failures, used to verify the executor stops execution and reports clear validation error messages.

nodes.name: config-test-variables.json
nodes.summary: Test fixture config that exercises the global variables feature, verifying that {{ .vars.variableName }} references resolve correctly across URLs, headers, and bodies.

nodes.name: .npmignore
nodes.summary: Specifies files and directories excluded from the published npm package, keeping development artifacts, docs, and tests out of the distributed tarball.

nodes.name: .understandignore
nodes.summary: Ignore-list controlling which files the Understand-Anything analyzer skips when scanning the project, mirroring gitignore-style patterns.

nodes.name: config.json
nodes.summary: Minimal configuration file for the Understand-Anything knowledge-graph tooling integrated with this project.

nodes.name: INTERNAL-TASKS.md
nodes.summary: Tracks internal/infrastructure improvements (tooling, debug mode, testing, code quality) that are not user-facing features, organized into 14 sections.

nodes.name: flowsphere.html
nodes.summary: Rendered Marp HTML slide deck presenting FlowSphere's purpose, features, and architecture for demos and onboarding.

nodes.name: flowsphere.md
nodes.summary: Marp markdown source for the FlowSphere presentation slide deck covering the tool's value proposition and capabilities across 14 sections.

nodes.name: flowsphere
nodes.summary: Deprecated Bash implementation of the HTTP sequence runner, providing the original full-featured CLI (substitution, conditions, validations, browser launch) before the Node.js rewrite.
nodes.languageNotes: Monolithic Bash script (~1846 lines) relying on jq/curl for JSON handling and HTTP; superseded by the modular Node.js lib/.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the deprecated Bash version of FlowSphere, its prerequisites (jq, curl), and migration guidance toward the Node.js implementation.

nodes.name: index.js
nodes.summary: Library barrel entry point that re-exports the public API and wraps executor.runSequence with simple run/runConfig helpers.
nodes.languageNotes: CommonJS module aggregating lib submodules into a single public surface.

nodes.name: run
nodes.summary: Loads a config file from disk and delegates to the executor's runSequence to run the HTTP workflow.

nodes.name: logger.js
nodes.summary: Execution logging module that interactively prompts to save runs and writes detailed JSON execution logs to the logs/ directory.

nodes.name: promptSaveLog
nodes.summary: Interactively prompts the user whether to save the execution log and triggers the save when confirmed.

nodes.name: saveLog
nodes.summary: Persists an execution log to a timestamped JSON file in the logs directory.

nodes.name: _saveLogWithMetadata
nodes.summary: Builds the full execution-log payload with metadata and step details and writes it to disk as formatted JSON.

nodes.name: fakeAPI-config.json
nodes.summary: Sample/fixture FlowSphere config generated for a fake API, used to exercise and demonstrate the Postman-to-FlowSphere converter output.

nodes.name: parse-postman.js
nodes.summary: Converter that reads a Postman collection plus environment, resolves variables and dependencies, and emits an optimized FlowSphere config with defaults, conditions, and validations.
nodes.languageNotes: Pure Node.js transformation pipeline; auto-detects step dependencies and rewires them with FlowSphere response-reference syntax.

nodes.name: generateNodeId
nodes.summary: Derives a stable, unique FlowSphere node id from a Postman request name, stripping numeric prefixes and normalizing characters.

nodes.name: replaceEnvVars
nodes.summary: Substitutes Postman environment variable references in a string with resolved values from the loaded environment.

nodes.name: buildUrlFromObject
nodes.summary: Reconstructs a request URL string from Postman's structured URL object (host, path, query).

nodes.name: convertAuthToHeaders
nodes.summary: Translates Postman auth definitions (bearer, basic, apikey) into FlowSphere request headers.

nodes.name: extractValidationsFromTests
nodes.summary: Parses Postman test scripts to infer FlowSphere validations such as status-code checks and JSON path assertions.

nodes.name: collectRequests
nodes.summary: Recursively walks Postman collection folders to collect all requests in numeric-prefix order.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Explains the Postman-to-FlowSphere converter: its capabilities, inputs, dependency auto-wiring, and how to run it.

nodes.name: site.webmanifest
nodes.summary: Web app manifest declaring favicon/icon metadata and PWA-style properties for the FlowSphere Studio browser app.

nodes.name: styles.css
nodes.summary: Comprehensive stylesheet for FlowSphere Studio, defining theme CSS variables (dark/light), layout, components, modals, and the visual config editor UI.
nodes.languageNotes: Uses CSS custom properties (--bg-surface, --text-primary, etc.) to drive theme-aware components per the project styling guide.

nodes.name: index.html
nodes.summary: Main HTML shell for FlowSphere Studio, defining the editor layout, modals, toolbars, and wiring all browser JS modules via global script tags.
nodes.languageNotes: Loads Bootstrap and all studio/js modules as global (non-ESM) scripts; functions communicate through the global window scope.

nodes.name: autocomplete.js
nodes.summary: Context-aware autocomplete engine for the {{ }} substitution syntax and jq expressions, including caret-position calculation and suggestion rendering across all Studio inputs.
nodes.languageNotes: Implements the mirror-div technique for accurate caret coordinates in textareas, as described in the project autocomplete docs.

nodes.name: buildAutocompleteSuggestions
nodes.summary: Builds the full set of context-aware suggestions for the {{ }} syntax including basic tokens, global variables, response references, and user input.

nodes.name: buildSchemaFieldSuggestionsForJq
nodes.summary: Generates jq path suggestions from response schema fields to assist users authoring jq expressions.

nodes.name: getCaretCoordinates
nodes.summary: Computes pixel coordinates of the text caret in inputs/textareas using a mirror-div, accounting for scrolling and CSS layout.

nodes.name: showAutocomplete
nodes.summary: Renders and positions the autocomplete dropdown at the caret with keyboard/mouse navigation support.

nodes.name: selectAutocompleteSuggestion
nodes.summary: Inserts the chosen suggestion into the target field, updating value and caret position.

nodes.name: attachAutocompleteToAllInputs
nodes.summary: Attaches autocomplete behavior to every relevant input and textarea across the Studio UI.

nodes.name: bootstrap-modal-bridge.js
nodes.summary: Bridges Studio's new-config and load-config modal workflows to Bootstrap modals, handling template selection and config creation/reset.

nodes.name: selectTemplate
nodes.summary: Handles selection of a config template (Empty, Simple, OAuth, User Input) in the new-config modal.

nodes.name: confirmNewConfig
nodes.summary: Creates a new config from the selected template, populating the editor state and re-rendering the Studio UI.

nodes.name: resetLoadConfigModal
nodes.summary: Resets the load-config modal's state and UI controls to a clean initial condition.

nodes.name: config-manager.js
nodes.summary: Manages the Studio config lifecycle: localStorage auto-save/load, file rename/download/close, and updating toolbar buttons and indicators.

nodes.name: renameFile
nodes.summary: Renames the current config file with validation and updates the displayed file name and stored state.

nodes.name: closeFile
nodes.summary: Closes the current config, clearing editor state, localStorage, and resetting the UI.

nodes.name: loadFromLocalStorage
nodes.summary: Restores a previously auto-saved config from browser localStorage into the editor on startup.

nodes.name: config-validator-ui.js
nodes.summary: Validates the in-editor config and renders validation results and status indicators in the Studio UI.

nodes.name: validateConfig
nodes.summary: Runs structural validation over the current config and collects errors/warnings for display.

nodes.name: showConfigValidationResults
nodes.summary: Renders the validation results panel with per-issue messages and pass/fail styling.

nodes.name: feature-registry.js
nodes.summary: Core toggleable-feature registry (IIFE module) that loads, enables, disables, and persists feature preferences, lazily injecting feature scripts on demand.
nodes.languageNotes: Module-pattern IIFE exposing a FeatureRegistry public API on the global scope; implements the project's toggleable-feature pattern.

nodes.name: loadFeature
nodes.summary: Lazily injects a feature's script tag and invokes its init function the first time the feature is enabled.

nodes.name: enableFeature
nodes.summary: Enables a feature, optionally persisting the preference and loading the feature script if not already loaded.

nodes.name: disableFeature
nodes.summary: Disables a feature and optionally persists the updated preference.

nodes.name: safe-wrappers.js
nodes.summary: Defines safe global wrapper functions that guard calls to optional/toggleable feature functions, no-oping gracefully when a feature is disabled or not loaded.
nodes.languageNotes: Wraps feature entrypoints in typeof checks so the UI never throws when a feature script is absent.

nodes.name: settings-ui.js
nodes.summary: Renders the Studio settings modal's Features tab and handles toggling features on/off, showing reload notifications via the feature registry.

nodes.name: renderFeaturesTab
nodes.summary: Builds the settings Features tab UI grouped by category, with toggles reflecting each feature's enabled state.

nodes.name: toggleFeature
nodes.summary: Enables or disables a feature via the registry and triggers UI adaptation and reload notifications as needed.

nodes.name: ui-adapter.js
nodes.summary: UIAdapter module that updates the Studio UI to reflect which toggleable features are currently loaded, debouncing rapid updates.
nodes.languageNotes: Exposes a debounced UIAdapter API on window; reconciles UI controls (theme toggle, Postman template, drag-drop) with feature load state.

nodes.name: updateUIForLoadedFeaturesImmediate
nodes.summary: Immediately reconciles Studio UI controls with the set of currently loaded toggleable features.

nodes.name: drag-drop-handler.js
nodes.summary: Implements drag-and-drop reordering of workflow steps in Studio, with drop indicators, auto-scroll, fade animations, and accordion-aware reordering logic.

nodes.name: initStepDragAndDrop
nodes.summary: Initializes drag-and-drop event listeners on the step accordion elements.

nodes.name: handleDrop
nodes.summary: Handles the drop event, computing target index and triggering the step reorder.

nodes.name: reorderSteps
nodes.summary: Reorders the config's nodes array to reflect the new step order after a drag-and-drop.

nodes.name: handleAutoScroll
nodes.summary: Auto-scrolls the step list while dragging near the top or bottom edge of the viewport.

nodes.name: flow-runner.js
nodes.summary: Browser-side Flow Runner that executes configuration sequences against the Express server over SSE, rendering a live execution monitor modal with step-by-step, auto-step, and full-speed modes, user-input prompts, substitution highlighting, and post-run log saving.
nodes.languageNotes: Global-script module (no ES imports); 51 top-level functions wiring DOM, SSE streaming, and the three execution modes (full-speed, step-by-step, auto-step).

nodes.name: runSequenceFullSpeed
nodes.summary: Drives a full-speed flow execution by streaming server-sent events from the backend, updating the monitor modal per step, handling user-input prompts and browser launches, and rendering validations and substitutions.

nodes.name: runSequenceStepByStep
nodes.summary: Executes the flow one step at a time, pausing after each step until the user clicks Continue, using a placeholder-driven gating mechanism.

nodes.name: runSequenceAutoStep
nodes.summary: Executes the flow automatically advancing between steps after a countdown delay, with a button to pause or skip the countdown.

nodes.name: showExecutionModeSelector
nodes.summary: Renders the execution-mode selection UI (full-speed, step-by-step, auto-step) before starting a flow run.

nodes.name: handleBrowserLaunch
nodes.summary: Extracts a URL from a step response via the launchBrowser jsonpath and triggers opening it in the user's browser during a flow run.

nodes.name: collectUserInputForStep
nodes.summary: Collects interactive user-input prompt values for a step during execution, resolving a promise once the user submits the form.

nodes.name: updateModalWithStep
nodes.summary: Updates the execution monitor modal with a completed step's request/response, validations, and substitution details.

nodes.name: displayExecutionResults
nodes.summary: Renders the final results summary of a completed flow run, including per-step outcomes and overall pass/fail status.

nodes.name: highlightSubstitutionsInText
nodes.summary: Highlights variable substitutions within a text payload for display, color-coding each substitution by its source type.

nodes.name: stopExecution
nodes.summary: Signals the server to gracefully interrupt the running flow and updates the monitor UI into a stopped state.

nodes.name: saveLogs
nodes.summary: Saves the execution log of the completed run by requesting the server to persist a timestamped JSON log file.

nodes.name: form-handlers.js
nodes.summary: Handles all form-driven edits to the config in Studio: adding/cloning/moving/removing nodes, editing default headers and validations, updating step JSON, and scrolling/highlighting the live JSON preview.
nodes.languageNotes: Global-script module mutating the shared config object then re-rendering via renderEditor/updatePreview.

nodes.name: addStep
nodes.summary: Adds a new node/step to the config, assigning a unique id and re-rendering the editor and JSON preview.

nodes.name: cloneStep
nodes.summary: Duplicates an existing node, generating a fresh unique id and inserting it after the original.

nodes.name: performStepSwap
nodes.summary: Reorders nodes by swapping two step positions in the config and re-rendering the editor.

nodes.name: updateStepJSON
nodes.summary: Parses raw JSON edited inline for a step and merges it back into the config, validating syntax before applying.

nodes.name: scrollToJsonSection
nodes.summary: Scrolls and highlights the relevant section of the live JSON preview corresponding to the field being edited.

nodes.name: renderDefaultValidations
nodes.summary: Renders the list of default validations defined in the config's defaults section with edit/remove controls.

nodes.name: copyJSON
nodes.summary: Copies the generated config JSON to the clipboard and shows a transient confirmation.

nodes.name: import-nodes.js
nodes.summary: Defines the ImportNodesModal class that fetches reusable node templates from the server, renders them by category in a modal, and imports selected templates into the config while resolving id conflicts and missing variables.
nodes.languageNotes: ES class exported as window.ImportNodesModal; the tree-sitter pass missed it because methods are class-body shorthand.

nodes.name: ImportNodesModal
nodes.summary: Manages the import-nodes workflow: fetching template categories, rendering template cards, and inserting chosen node templates into the config while renaming conflicting ids and creating missing global variables.

nodes.name: addNodesToConfig
nodes.summary: Appends imported template nodes to the config's nodes array and triggers re-render, preview update, and localStorage save.

nodes.name: renameConflictingIds
nodes.summary: Detects node id collisions against the existing config and renames imported nodes to keep ids unique.

nodes.name: detectMissingVariables
nodes.summary: Scans imported nodes for referenced global variables not yet defined in the config so they can be auto-created.

nodes.name: main.js
nodes.summary: Bootstrap and wiring entry point for FlowSphere Studio that initializes the editor on load, restores UI state from localStorage, and toggles the JSON preview pane.
nodes.languageNotes: Global-script bootstrap; relies on functions defined across the other studio/js modules.

nodes.name: toggleJsonPreview
nodes.summary: Shows or hides the live JSON preview pane and persists the visibility preference to localStorage.

nodes.name: restoreJsonPreviewState
nodes.summary: Restores the JSON preview pane's open/closed state from localStorage on page load.

nodes.name: modals.js
nodes.summary: Houses all Studio editor modals and list renderers for conditions, validations, user prompts, and headers, including their show/save/remove handlers and dynamic source/type field rendering.
nodes.languageNotes: Global-script module; 38 functions following a consistent render/add/edit/show/save/remove/close pattern per editable collection.

nodes.name: showConditionModal
nodes.summary: Opens the condition editor modal, rendering source and type fields for adding or editing a conditional-execution rule on a node.

nodes.name: saveCondition
nodes.summary: Validates and persists a condition from the modal into the node's conditions array, then re-renders the list and preview.

nodes.name: showValidationModal
nodes.summary: Opens the validation editor modal supporting HTTP status code and jsonpath validation types with multiple comparison criteria.

nodes.name: saveValidation
nodes.summary: Collects and stores a validation rule (status code or jsonpath with criteria) into the node's validations array.

nodes.name: renderConditionSourceFields
nodes.summary: Renders the dynamic input fields for a condition based on the selected source (node, variable, or input).

nodes.name: showPromptModal
nodes.summary: Opens the user-prompt editor modal for defining interactive userPrompts on a node.

nodes.name: showHeaderModal
nodes.summary: Opens the header editor modal for adding or editing a request header on a node.

nodes.name: postman-parser.js
nodes.summary: Client-side parser that converts an uploaded Postman collection (with environment variables) into a FlowSphere config, sorting requests, resolving variables, and auto-wiring response dependencies.
nodes.languageNotes: Single large function parsePostmanCollection encapsulating the full collection-to-config transform.

nodes.name: parsePostmanCollection
nodes.summary: Parses a Postman collection JSON into a FlowSphere config: extracts requests, resolves environment variables, generates defaults, and maps Postman dynamic variables to FlowSphere substitution syntax.

nodes.name: postman-preview.js
nodes.summary: Renders a preview modal of a parsed Postman import showing the resulting nodes, variables, auth, and validations before the user confirms importing into the config.
nodes.languageNotes: Global-script module populating preview sections from the parser output.

nodes.name: confirmPostmanImport
nodes.summary: Applies the previewed Postman import into the active config and re-renders the editor and JSON preview.

nodes.name: populatePreviewNodes
nodes.summary: Populates the preview modal's node list showing each request that will be imported with method and URL.

nodes.name: state.js
nodes.summary: Defines the global config object that is the single source of truth for the Studio editor, plus a helper for the empty-state placeholder HTML.
nodes.languageNotes: Holds the shared mutable config global; consumed by every other studio/js module without ES imports.

nodes.name: getEmptyStateHTML
nodes.summary: Returns the placeholder HTML shown when no nodes exist in the config yet.

nodes.name: templates.js
nodes.summary: Provides starter config templates (Empty, Simple, OAuth, User Input) returned by name for quick config creation in Studio.
nodes.languageNotes: Single getTemplate function returning predefined config objects by key.

nodes.name: getTemplate
nodes.summary: Returns a predefined config template object for the requested template name used to seed a new config.

nodes.name: theme-switcher.js
nodes.summary: Toggles and persists the Studio light/dark theme, applying the saved preference on load.

nodes.name: setTheme
nodes.summary: Applies the given theme to the document and saves the preference to localStorage.

nodes.name: try-it-out.js
nodes.summary: Implements the per-node Try it Out feature: detecting dependencies, prompting for mock responses, executing a single node against the server, displaying results, and inferring/merging response schemas.
nodes.languageNotes: IIFE-wrapped module (functions indented inside the closure) which is why the tree-sitter pass reported zero functions.

nodes.name: executeNode
nodes.summary: Executes a single node against the Express server using provided mock responses and returns the request/response result.

nodes.name: showMockingModal
nodes.summary: Renders a modal letting the user supply mock responses for the dependencies a node references before running it in isolation.

nodes.name: detectDependencies
nodes.summary: Scans a node for response-reference substitutions to determine which prior-step responses must be mocked for a standalone run.

nodes.name: extractSchema
nodes.summary: Infers a JSON schema shape from a response value recursively, used to capture and store response schemas per node.

nodes.name: showResultsModal
nodes.summary: Displays the Try it Out execution result in a modal with request, response, validations, and substitutions, offering to capture the response schema.

nodes.name: mergeSchemas
nodes.summary: Merges a newly inferred response schema with an existing stored schema for a node.

nodes.name: storeResponseSchema
nodes.summary: Persists a node's captured response schema into the config, optionally merging with a prior schema.

nodes.name: ui-renderer-bootstrap.js
nodes.summary: Bootstrap-themed renderer for the Studio editor: renders the full editor, global variables, default headers, response schemas, step list and step forms, and floating add-node controls.
nodes.languageNotes: Provides renderEditor and renderSteps — the central re-render entry points invoked across the studio modules.

nodes.name: renderEditor
nodes.summary: Top-level render entry point that rebuilds the entire editor UI from the current config state.

nodes.name: renderSteps
nodes.summary: Renders the list of step/node cards with method badges, drag handles, and per-step action controls.

nodes.name: renderStepForm
nodes.summary: Renders the editable form for a single node including method, URL, headers, body, conditions, and validations.

nodes.name: renderResponseSchemas
nodes.summary: Renders the captured response schemas section showing stored schemas per node with delete controls.

nodes.name: initFloatingAddNodeButtons
nodes.summary: Initializes the floating add-node buttons that let users insert a node between existing steps.

nodes.name: ui-renderer.js
nodes.summary: Legacy/base renderer providing core editor rendering functions (renderEditor, renderSteps, renderStepForm, global variables and default headers) for the Studio editor.
nodes.languageNotes: Parallel, simpler implementation of the renderer alongside ui-renderer-bootstrap.js.

nodes.name: renderEditor
nodes.summary: Base render entry point that rebuilds the editor UI from the current config in the non-Bootstrap renderer.

nodes.name: renderStepForm
nodes.summary: Renders the editable form for a single node in the base renderer.

nodes.name: README.md
nodes.summary: Documents the FlowSphere Studio plug-and-play architecture: module structure, core vs optional features, load order, dynamic loading, graceful degradation, and how to manage/disable features.

nodes.name: premium-user.json
nodes.summary: Reusable conditional node template that executes only for premium users, demonstrating condition-based execution with variable substitution.

nodes.name: skip-on-error.json
nodes.summary: Reusable conditional node template that skips execution when a prior step returned an error, demonstrating status-code-based conditional flow.

layers.name: CLI Entry
layers.description: Command-line entry point that parses arguments, routes commands, and launches the Express server hosting FlowSphere Studio.

layers.name: Execution Engine
layers.description: Core Node.js library that orchestrates sequential HTTP request execution: variable substitution, validation, conditional execution, HTTP client, logging, and config validation.

layers.name: FlowSphere Studio UI
layers.description: Browser-based visual config editor: HTML/CSS shell, app modules (state, renderers, flow-runner, try-it-out, modals, import, autocomplete), node templates, and assets.

layers.name: Studio Feature Infrastructure
layers.description: Feature-toggle infrastructure for Studio: feature registry, settings UI, UI adapter, and safe wrappers that make UI capabilities optional and configurable.

layers.name: Postman Integration
layers.description: Converts Postman collections and environments into FlowSphere config files, including the parser, sample fixtures, and generated config.

layers.name: Example Configurations
layers.description: Ready-to-run example workflow JSON configs demonstrating authentication, user input, OAuth, and comprehensive feature usage.

layers.name: Tests & Fixtures
layers.description: Test fixture configs and the validator test suite exercising validations, conditions, defaults merging, and error handling.

layers.name: Documentation & Project Meta
layers.description: Project documentation (features, technical, implementation, prompts, presentation), root README/ROADMAP/CLAUDE guides, the deprecated Bash legacy version, and project-level manifest/meta config.

tour.title: Project Overview
tour.description: Start here. The README introduces FlowSphere's core promise: "Design in Studio. Execute Anywhere." It explains that the tool automates multi-step API workflows where each step reuses data from previous responses (login then get-profile then create-resource), and that the same engine powers both a CLI executor and a visual browser Studio. Read this to understand the configuration format, the {{ }} substitution syntax, and the two interfaces before diving into code.

tour.title: Package Manifest & CLI Entry
tour.description: FlowSphere is distributed as an npm package. package.json declares the `flowsphere` bin pointing at bin/flowsphere.js and the three runtime dependencies that define the whole system: axios (HTTP), express (the Studio server), and open (browser launch). bin/flowsphere.js is the entry point reached by both `node bin/flowsphere.js config.json` and the global `flowsphere` command, and it has the highest fan-in among code files because everything starts here.
tour.languageLesson: In package.json the `bin` field maps a command name to a script with a `#!/usr/bin/env node` shebang. When the package is installed globally, npm creates an executable shim so typing `flowsphere` runs that file directly.

tour.title: The Execution Engine Core
tour.description: bin/flowsphere.js delegates the actual work to lib/executor.js, the heart of the system. This orchestrator loads and validates the config, merges per-step settings with `defaults`, collects user input, evaluates conditions, fires each HTTP request, validates the response, and maintains a `responses[]` array so later steps can reference earlier results. This is the single engine the README promises runs identically from CLI and Studio.
tour.languageLesson: The executor keeps an in-memory `responses[]` array as accumulating state across an otherwise sequential async loop. Because each `await`ed step completes before the next begins, named references like {{ .responses.login.token }} are always resolvable by the time a later step runs.

tour.title: Variable Substitution
tour.description: Building on the executor's `responses[]` state from Step 3, lib/substitution.js is what makes data flow between steps. It replaces the four placeholder families described in the README: dynamic values ({{ $guid }}, {{ $timestamp }}), global variables ({{ .vars.* }}), user input ({{ .input.* }}), and response references ({{ .responses.* }}). Substitution runs recursively through strings, objects, and arrays in a fixed order, so a single request body can weave together a fresh UUID, a config secret, and a token from a prior step.
tour.languageLesson: Recursive descent over a JSON value (string vs object vs array) is the idiomatic way to apply a transform everywhere in a nested structure. The substitution order is deliberate: dynamic placeholders first, then vars, input, and finally response references.

tour.title: Validation & Conditional Execution
tour.description: After each request, two engines decide what happens next. lib/validator.js checks the response against a node's rules (HTTP status code plus jsonpath criteria like exists, equals, greaterThan) and stops the run on failure. lib/conditions.js, evaluated *before* a step runs, decides whether to execute or skip it based on previous responses, variables, or input, combining multiple checks with AND logic. Together they turn a flat list of requests into a guarded, branching workflow.

tour.title: HTTP Client & Shared Utilities
tour.description: These two depth-2 modules are the foundation the rest of the engine stands on. lib/http-client.js wraps axios with timeout handling (seconds to milliseconds), automatic JSON parsing, and network-error reporting. lib/utils.js is the most depended-upon file in the codebase (highest fan-in): it provides UUID and timestamp generation for substitution, the jsonpath extraction used by validators and conditions, deep-merge for defaults, and terminal colorization. Almost every other lib module imports it.

tour.title: Config Validation & Public API
tour.description: Before any request fires, lib/config-validator.js statically checks the entire config: top-level shape, variables, defaults, node ids/methods/urls, conditions, validation rules, and malformed {{ }} placeholders. This catches mistakes early in both CLI and Studio. lib/index.js is the library barrel that re-exports the public API and wraps `executor.runSequence` with simple `run`/`runConfig` helpers, so FlowSphere can be embedded programmatically as described in the README.
tour.languageLesson: A barrel file (index.js that only re-exports) defines a clean public surface for a module folder, letting consumers `require('flowsphere')` without reaching into individual lib files.

tour.title: The Config Format by Example
tour.description: Now that you know the engine, see what it consumes. config-simple.json is the minimal starter: it hits the public JSONPlaceholder API with just defaults and validations, no setup required. config-comprehensive-demo.json is the opposite end: a 12-node workflow exercising every feature at once (global variables, user prompts, conditional execution, all validation types, dynamic placeholders, all HTTP methods). Reading these makes the abstract format from Step 1 concrete.
tour.languageLesson: FlowSphere configs are pure declarative JSON: a `defaults` block reduces duplication (baseUrl, common headers, default validations) and each `nodes[]` entry overrides defaults as needed. Behavior lives entirely in data, not code.

tour.title: Studio: Shell & State
tour.description: FlowSphere's second interface is the browser Studio, served by the Express server launched in Step 2. studio/index.html is the editor shell defining the layout, modals, and the script tags that wire every browser module. studio/js/state.js holds the single source of truth: a global `config` object that all editing mutates. The architecture docs stress that config lives here in state.js (never `window.config`), and the UI re-renders after every change.

tour.title: Same Engine, Two Interfaces: SSE Flow Runner
tour.description: This is the README's key claim made real. studio/js/flow-runner.js does NOT reimplement execution; it streams the config to the Express server, which runs the exact same lib/executor.js engine from Steps 3-6, and pushes per-step results back over Server-Sent Events. The browser renders a live monitor with step-by-step, auto-step, and full-speed modes plus substitution highlighting. The companion architecture doc traces this client-server execution lifecycle end to end.
tour.languageLesson: Server-Sent Events (SSE) is a one-way HTTP streaming protocol: the server holds the response open and emits `data:` events as each step finishes. This is simpler than WebSockets when the browser only needs to receive a live progress feed.

tour.title: Try It Out & Toggleable Features
tour.description: Studio also lets you execute a single node in isolation. studio/js/try-it-out.js detects a node's dependencies, prompts for mock upstream responses, runs just that node against the server, and infers a response schema, again reusing the shared engine. studio/js/core/feature-registry.js underpins FlowSphere's design rule that new capabilities are toggleable: it loads, enables, disables, and persists feature preferences, lazily injecting feature scripts on demand.
tour.languageLesson: The feature registry is an IIFE (immediately-invoked function expression) module: it wraps its state in a private scope and exposes only a small API, the classic browser pattern for encapsulation without a module bundler.

tour.title: Postman Integration
tour.description: To lower the barrier to adoption, FlowSphere can import existing Postman collections. postman-tools/parse-postman.js reads a Postman collection plus environment, resolves variables, auto-detects dependencies between requests, and emits an optimized FlowSphere config with `defaults`, conditions, and validations already wired up using {{ .responses.* }} syntax. Its README documents the capabilities and how to run the converter.

tour.title: Tests & Roadmap
tour.description: Finish with how the project guards quality and where it's heading. tests/test-validator-categories.js verifies that config validation correctly separates structure errors from value errors across full, structure-only, and values-only modes, and tests/README.md indexes the fixture configs that exercise variables, validations, defaults, and comparisons. ROADMAP.md then lays out completed and planned features (OpenAPI import, OAuth callback capture, multi-environment variable groups), giving you a map of the codebase's future.
